title: Microsoft Azure Online Training Program
content:
Introduction to Microsoft Azure Online Training Program

Welcome to our Microsoft Azure Online Training Program, designed to provide participants with a deep understanding of cloud computing, Azure services, and real-world applications of Microsoft Azure. It covers three key certifications: AZ-900, AZ-104, AZ-204, and AZ-400, ensuring a comprehensive learning experience. With live projects, interactive sessions, and hands-on exercises, this training program will prepare you to become proficient in Azure fundamentals, administration, development, and DevOps.

Whether you’re a beginner looking to understand the fundamentals of cloud computing or an experienced IT professional seeking to enhance your skills with Azure certifications, our training program is designed for all levels.

New Batch: Coming Soon

Registration Link: https://forms.gle/vxgePayr5jxBbZbTA
Join the Microsoft Azure Telegram Group for This Batch: https://telegram.me/+OULrgzs6Ibs0YTRl
Contact us through our WhatsApp Number: +91 7021801173
Call us for More Details: +91 7021801173
Contact us through Our Email: onlinetraining@example.com

What is Microsoft Azure?

Microsoft Azure is a leading cloud computing platform and service provided by Microsoft. With over 200 services and multiple regions worldwide, Microsoft Azure enables businesses to build, deploy, and manage applications through Microsoft-managed data centers. It supports various programming languages, tools, and frameworks, making it one of the most widely used cloud platforms in the industry. Microsoft Azure provides a wide range of services, including:

  • Compute Services: Virtual machines, containers, and serverless computing.
  • Storage Solutions: Secure and scalable storage options for data, files, and backups.
  • Networking: Virtual networks, load balancers, and gateways for secure connectivity.
  • Databases: Managed SQL and NoSQL databases, including Cosmos DB.
  • Security and Identity: Services like Azure Active Directory for identity management and access control.
Why Should You Learn Microsoft Azure?

Cloud computing has revolutionized the way businesses operate, and Azure is one of the most trusted and widely used platforms across various industries. Learning Microsoft Azure opens up career opportunities in cloud architecture, development, security, and administration. With cloud adoption continuing to grow, expertise in Azure can enhance your career prospects, help you transition to cloud-based roles, and keep you ahead in the competitive IT industry. The following are some of the reasons why You Should Learn Microsoft Azure:

  • High Demand for Azure Professionals: With over 200 products and services, Azure dominates the cloud market alongside AWS and Google Cloud.
  • Career Opportunities: Azure certifications enhance your credibility and open up job opportunities in cloud administration, development, and architecture.
  • Future-Proof Skills: Cloud computing is rapidly evolving, and proficiency in Azure ensures your skills remain relevant.
  • Enterprise Adoption: Most Fortune 500 companies utilize Azure, making it a crucial skill for IT professionals.
Microsoft Azure Online Training Program Objectives

The primary goal of our Microsoft Azure Online Training Program is to give participants an in-depth understanding of Microsoft Azure’s core services and their applications in real-world scenarios. You will gain hands-on experience by working on live projects, preparing you to effectively utilize Azure for various tasks, including app development, infrastructure management, identity management, and security management. So, at the end of this Microsoft Azure Online Training Program, participants will:

  • Gain a solid understanding of cloud computing concepts.
  • Learn to implement, manage, and secure Azure services.
  • Acquire hands-on experience in deploying Azure resources and solutions.
  • Understand Azure administration and development.
  • Prepare for Microsoft Azure certifications: AZ-900, AZ-104, AZ-204, and AZ-400.
Who Should Enroll in This Microsoft Azure Online Training Program?

This training program is ideal for IT professionals, software developers, system administrators, solution architects, and anyone seeking to deepen their understanding of cloud computing. It’s also highly beneficial for individuals preparing for Microsoft Azure certification exams, including AZ-900, AZ-104, AZ-204, and AZ-400.

How to Join Our Microsoft Azure Online Training Program?

Please fill out the form to join our Microsoft Azure Online Training Program. Once you have filled it out, we will contact you and share the details about the training program. You can contact us through our Mobile/WhatsApp number (+91 7021801173) and Email ID (onlinetraining@example.com).

Topic: Microsoft Azure (AZ900, AZ104, AZ204, and AZ400)

Azure Fundamentals – AZ900 Course Syllabus

Chapter-1: Introduction to Cloud Computing
  • What is a Server, how does a Server room look, and what are the Different types of Servers
  • What is Cloud Computing, and its evolution? How it destroyed the on-premise solutions. Advantages of Cloud over on-premise.
  • Types of Cloud (Public/Private/Hybrid), Types of Cloud services (IAAS, PAAS, SAAS)
  • Capex vs. Opex
  • Describe the shared responsibility model
  • Describe the Consumption-Based Model in Azure
Chapter-2: Introduction to Azure
  • What is Azure? Who are Azure’s competitors, and what is its current market share?
  • What are Regions, Availability zones, and Data centers? How are they internally connected?
  • Describe Azure regions, region pairs, and sovereign regions
  • Describe the Hierarchy of Management: groups, resource groups, and subscription groups
  • What happens when you create a component/service in the Azure environment?
  • Different Components of Azure (Common cloud services)
  • Compare cloud pricing models in Azure
  • How to create your first Microsoft Azure account and build some sample Azure components/services
Chapter-3: Azure Identity and Security Management
  • Difference between Tenant, EntraID, Subscription, and Management Group
  • Create a User Account, Create a Group, and Enable MFA (Multi-factor Authentication), passwordless
  • Describe external identities in Azure, including business-to-business (B2B) and business-to-customer (B2C)
  • Describe directory services in Azure, including Microsoft Entra ID and Microsoft Entra Domain Services
  • What is Azure Active Directory? What is the Difference between Active Directory and Azure Active Directory
  • What are Azure Subscriptions, and what are the different types of subscriptions?
  • What is SSO in Azure
  • What is Conditional Access in Azure AD and the zero trust model in Azure
  • What is Azure Defender/Azure Security Center
  • Describe the purpose of the defense-in-depth model
  • Azure Key Vault and where it is used
  • What is Azure DDOS Protection?
  • What is Azure Firewall and Azure Application Gateway
  • Routing Rules in Application Gateway
  • Azure Role-based Access Control (RBAC)
  • What are Azure Management Groups? How does the Management Group work?
  • What are Azure Service Health, Azure Advisor, and Azure Traffic Manager?
Chapter-4: Azure Cost Management
  • What is Composite SLA, and how does Composite SLA work? Pricing and Support – Composite SLA
  • What is TCO (Total Cost of Ownership)? What does it do?
  • What is Azure Marketplace? How is it helpful for you? What is Azure Licensing?
  • Describe factors that can affect costs and cost management capabilities in Azure.
  • What is the Azure Pricing Calculator, and Azure Cost Management?
  • What are Resource Tags? Explain with an Example.
Chapter-5: Azure Governance
  • What is Microsoft’s Purview?
  • What are Azure Blueprints?
  • What is the purpose of Azure Policy and resource locks?
Chapter-6: Azure Management and Deployment
  • Describe the Azure portal
  • Describe the purpose of Azure Arc and what Infrastructure as Code (IAC)
  • What is Azure Cloud Shell/Azure PowerShell/Azure CLI?
  • Describe Azure Resource Manager (ARM) and ARM templates
  • Service Level Agreements and Service Credits
Chapter-7: Azure Compute Services
  • Introduction to Azure VMs, Create a VM, and understand its components
  • What are Azure Scalesets, availability sets, and their application? What is Azure Virtual Desktop
  • What are Azure app services and serverless computing? Difference between App Service and VM. How to create a web app.
  • Azure Container instances
Chapter-8: Azure Storage
  • What is Azure Storage? Different types of Azure Storage
  • Introduction to Azure Storage account, /Access Tiers/
  • What is LRS/ZRS/GZRS (Redundancy options)
  • Describe Storage account options and storage types.
  • Difference between Azure Blob storage and ADLS Gen2
  • What are Block blobs/Page blobs/Append blobs
  • How to Install AZcopy in Microsoft and macOS, and What is the use of AZCopy with an example
  • What is a SAS Token, and how to generate a SAS Token
  • What are Azure files, and how are they different from Azure Blob storage
  • What is Azure Storage Explorer, and how do you install it?
  • What is Azure File Sync, and how is it different from Azure Files
  • What is Shared File Access
  • What are Azure Cloud Adoption Framework, Azure Migrate, and Azure DataBox
Chapter-9: Azure Networking
  • What is Azure Virtual Networking? Explanation with an example.
  • What are Azure DNS/Route tables/Address spaces
  • What is ExpressRoute and Private Links
  • Define Private and Public endpoints
  • Azure Virtual WAN/ Azure Virtual Network Gateway
  • What are Network interfaces and VNet peering
  • What is a Subnet, Private/Public Subnet, VPN
Chapter-10: Azure Monitoring
  • Introduction To Azure Monitoring
  • Log Analytics Workspaces
  • Describe the purpose of Azure Advisor
  • Azure Monitor Alerts: When Are They Useful?
  • What are Application Insights?
  • Describe Azure Service Health

Azure Administrator – AZ104 Course Syllabus

Chapter-1: Microsoft Entra ID Management
  • How to Create Entra ID and Switching between Entra ID Tenants
  • How do you create users/invite external users and groups? How to manage user and group properties.
  • What is Entra ID Free vs Premium
  • Manage Licenses in Microsoft Entra ID and Different Types of Entra ID.
  • How can you upgrade your free trial to a premium P2
  • Configure Self-Service Password Reset
  • How to add custom Domains
  • What are Administrative Units and Managing Devices
Chapter-2: Resource and Access Management
  • Overview of RBAC and managing resources with roles.
  • Manage Resource groups and Subscriptions.
  • What is Azure Policy, and how can it be implemented and managed? How to Create a Custom Policy
  • Manage costs by using alerts, budgets, and Azure Advisor recommendations
  • How to configure Management groups
Chapter-3: Azure Storage Management
  • How to create a Storage account with the advanced options
  • Configure Azure Storage firewalls and virtual networks
  • Create and use shared access signature (SAS) tokens and Manage Access Keys
  • Storage Account Data Protection and Encryption
  • What are Blobs, Files, Queues, and Tables
  • Access Tiers and Data Redundancy
  • Manage data by using Azure Storage Explorer and AzCopy
  • Create and configure a file share in Azure Storage and a container in Blob Storage
  • Configure snapshots and soft delete for Azure Files
  • Configure blob lifecycle management and blob versioning
Chapter-4: Virtual Machines and Containers
  • Create a virtual machine
  • VM disk options and VM networking options
  • Configure Azure Disk Encryption
  • Manage virtual machine sizes and virtual machine disks
  • Deploy virtual machines to availability zones and availability sets
  • Deploy and configure an Azure Virtual Machine Scale Set
  • Create and manage an Azure container registry
  • Provision a container by using Azure Container Instances
Chapter-5: App Service Management
  • Create an App Service
  • Provision an App Service plan and Configure scaling for an App Service plan
  • Webapps deployment options and what is Auto-scaling in web apps
  • Configure certificates and Transport Layer Security (TLS) for an App Service
  • Map an existing custom DNS name to an App Service
  • Backup and network settings of an app service
Chapter-6: Virtual Networking Management
  • Create and configure virtual networks and subnets
  • Create and configure virtual network peering and Global Peering
  • What are Public IP Addresses and NICs
  • Create and configure network security groups (NSGs) and application security groups.
  • Evaluate effective security rules in NSGs
  • Implement Azure Bastion
  • Configure service endpoints for the Azure platform as a service (PaaS)
  • Configure private endpoints for Azure PaaS
  • Open Ports to Allow RDP Access, Add a Firewall to the Network
  • Network Routing, Add Firewall Rules
  • Virtual WAN
  • How to Configure Global Peering, what is Virtual Network Gateway
  • Configure Azure DNS
  • Configure an internal or public load balancer and Troubleshoot Load Balancer.
Chapter-7: ARM Templates
  • Interpret an Azure Resource Manager template or a Bicep file
  • Modify an existing Azure Resource Manager template
  • Modify an existing Bicep file
  • Deploy resources by using an Azure Resource Manager template or a Bicep file.
Chapter-8: Monitoring and Backup
  • Interpret metrics in Azure Monitor
  • Configure log settings in Azure Monitor
  • Query and analyze logs in Azure Monitor
  • How to set up alert rules, action groups, and alert processing rules in Azure Monitor
  • Configure and interpret monitoring of virtual machines, storage accounts, and networks by using Azure Monitor Insights
  • Azure Network Watcher and Connection Monitor
  • Create an Azure Backup and configure the backup policy
  • Perform backup and restore operations by using Azure Backup
  • and Configure Azure Site Recovery for Azure resources
  • Perform a failover to a secondary region by using Site Recovery
  • Configure and interpret reports and alerts for backups

Azure Developer Associate – AZ204 Course Syllabus

Chapter-1: Azure App Service Development
  • How do you create a web app and publish an app service with app service plans?
  • Manual Scaling VS Auto-scaling in App Service
  • Configure deployment slots,
  • Configure settings, including Transport Layer Security (TLS), API settings, and service connections
  • App Service SSL settings, Enabling Diagnostic Logs
  • Create a Web app in PowerShell/CLI
Chapter-2: Azure Containers
  • What is Azure Container Instance (ACI)? Create and manage container images for solutions.
  • Publish an image to Azure Container Registry
  • Using Visual Studio to Build a Container App
  • How to deploy an Azure Web App from Azure Container Registry
  • Running containers using Azure Container Instances
Chapter-3: Azure Functions Development
  • Creating a function app, building your first Hello World function
  • Implement function triggers by using data operations, timers, and webhooks
  • Create a Durable Function
  • Monitoring Function Events and HTTP trigger functions
Chapter-4: Cosmos DB Development
  • Overview of CosmosDB, Setting Up a CosmosDB Account, Global Distribution and Replication Features
  • CosmosDB Keys and Security, create a CosmosDB Collection, and Add Documents to CosmosDB.
  • Configuring Data Consistency Levels in CosmosDB
  • Configure and access properties and metadata.
  • Execute data operations using the relevant software development kit (SDK).
  • Apply storage policies and manage the data lifecycle.
Chapter-5: Authentication and Authorization
  • Use the Microsoft Identity platform to authenticate and authorize users.
  • Leverage Microsoft Entra ID for user and app authentication and authorization.
  • Generate and implement shared access signatures.
  • Develop solutions that integrate with Microsoft Graph.
Chapter-6: Secure App Configuration and Encryption
  • Secure app configuration data by using App Configuration or Azure Key Vault
  • Develop code that uses keys, secrets, and certificates stored in Azure Key Vault
  • Implement Managed Identities for Azure resources
  • Secure App Configuration
  • Data Encryption with Storage Accounts
  • Data Encryption with SQL Databases
  • Configuring Customer-Managed Key (CMK) with Azure Key Vault
Chapter-7: Caching
  • Overview of Redis Caching
  • Interacting with Redis: Reading and Writing Data in .NET
  • Redis Premium Cluster Configuration
  • Setting Up Cache and Expiration Policies in Azure Cache for Redis
  • Design and Implement Secure, Efficient Caching Patterns: Data Sizing, Connections, Encryption, and Expiration
  • Deploy and Configure Azure Content Delivery Network (CDN) Endpoints and Profiles
Chapter-8: Monitoring and Diagnostics
  • Track and analyze metrics, logs, and traces.
  • Set up Application Insights web tests and configure alerts.
  • Integrate an app or service with Application Insights for monitoring.
Chapter-9: API Management
  • Set up an Azure API Management instance.
  • Design and document APIs.
  • Configure API access permissions.
  • Apply policies to manage APIs.
Chapter-10: Event-Based Solutions
  • Develop solutions leveraging Azure Event Grid.
  • Build solutions utilizing Azure Event Hub.
  • Develop solutions that integrate with Azure Service Bus.
  • Build solutions that utilize Azure Queue Storage.

Azure DevOps – AZ400 Course Syllabus

Chapter-1: Introduction to DevOps 
  • What is DevOps? Understanding the core principles of DevOps, including collaboration, automation, and continuous improvement. 
  • Introduction to Software Development Life Cycle & its components. 
  • Overview of Agile Methodology and Scrum Processes. 
  • Exploring the benefits of DevOps, such as increased deployment frequency, improved lead time, and enhanced customer satisfaction. 
  • How do Modern DevOps principles overcome traditional software development approaches? 
  • Listing of different project management tools, Version Control, and CI/CD providers. 
Chapter-2: Design and implement a structured workflow with end-to-end visibility. 
  • Learn to create an organized workflow for managing tasks and code using GitHub Flow for team collaboration. 
  • Develop a system for collecting and responding to feedback through notifications and GitHub Issues. 
  • Utilize tools such as GitHub Projects, Azure Boards, and repositories to track work progress and streamline project management. 
  • Establish systems to track source code changes, identify bugs, and monitor quality metrics throughout the development process. 
Chapter-3:  Design and implement appropriate metrics and queries for DevOps. 
  • Design and implement a dashboard to track key metrics, including cycle time, time to recovery, and lead time. 
  • Define and implement metrics and queries for effective project planning and management. 
  • Set up metrics and queries to monitor development progress, quality, and efficiency. 
  • Implement metrics and queries for tracking testing performance, coverage, and defect rates. 
  • Develop metrics and queries to measure and monitor security risks, vulnerabilities, and compliance. 
  • Design metrics and queries to track delivery performance, including deployment frequency and release quality. 
  • Set up metrics and queries to monitor operational performance, system reliability, and incident response. 
Chapter-4:  Configure collaborations and communications for DevOps. 
  • Learn to document projects using wikis and process diagrams with Markdown and Mermaid syntax. 
  • Configure release documentation, including generating release notes and API docs. 
  • Automate the creation of documentation from Git history. 
  • Set up integration using webhooks. 
  • Integrate Azure Boards with GitHub repositories. 
  • Configure integration between GitHub or Azure DevOps and Microsoft Teams. 
Chapter-5:  Design and implement branching strategies for the source code 
  • Design a branch strategy incorporating trunk-based, feature, and release branches. 
  • Implement a pull request workflow using branch policies and protections. 
  • Set up branch merging restrictions with branch policies and protections. 
  • Design a strategy for scaling and optimizing Git repositories with tools like Scalar and cross-repository sharing. 
  • Configure permissions within the source control repository. 
  • Use tags to organize and manage the source control repository. 
  • Recover specific data using Git commands. 
  • Remove specific data from source control when needed. 
Chapter-6:  Design and implement a package management strategy 
  • Recommend package management tools like GitHub Packages and Azure Artifacts. 
  • Design and implement package feeds and views for managing local and upstream packages. 
  • Develop a dependency versioning strategy for code and packages that utilizes Semantic Versioning (SemVer) and Calendar Versioning (CalVer). 
  • Design a versioning strategy for pipeline artifacts. 
Chapter-7:  Design and implement a testing strategy for pipelines 
  • Design and implement quality and release gates that incorporate security and governance controls. 
  • Develop a comprehensive testing strategy that encompasses local, unit, integration, and load tests. 
  • Integrate tests into the pipeline, including configuring test tasks, agents, and test result reporting. 
  • Implement code coverage analysis to track the effectiveness of tests. 
Chapter-8:  Design and implement pipelines and deployments 
  • Choose a deployment automation solution, such as GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines. 
  • Design and implement infrastructure for GitHub runners or Azure DevOps agents, taking into account cost, tool selection, licenses, connectivity, and maintainability. 
  • Integrate GitHub repositories with Azure Pipelines. 
  • Develop pipeline trigger rules and configure pipelines using YAML. 
  • Plan and implement job execution order, including parallelism and multi-stage pipelines. 
  • Develop complex pipeline scenarios, including hybrid pipelines, virtual machine (VM) templates, and self-hosted runners or agents. 
  • Create reusable pipeline components, such as YAML templates, task groups, and variable groups, to streamline your workflow. 
  • Implement checks and approvals using YAML-based environments. 
  • Design deployment strategies, including blue-green, canary, ring, progressive exposure, feature flags, and A/B testing. 
  • Ensure reliable dependency deployment ordering in pipelines. 
  • Plan for minimizing downtime during deployments by utilizing techniques such as VIP swap, load balancing, and rolling deployments. 
  • Design a hotfix plan for high-priority code fixes. 
  • Implement a resiliency strategy for deployment processes. 
  • Use the Azure App Configuration Feature Manager to implement feature flags. 
  • Implement application deployment using containers, binaries, and scripts. 
  • Handle database tasks during deployment. 
Chapter-9:  Design and implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) 
  • Recommend a configuration management tool for application infrastructure. 
  • Implement a configuration management strategy for managing application infrastructure. 
  • Define an Infrastructure as Code (IaC) strategy, including source control, testing, and deployment automation. 
  • Design and implement desired state configurations for environments using tools like Azure Automation State Configuration, Azure Resource Manager, Bicep, and Azure Automanage Machine Configuration. 
  • Design and implement Azure Deployment Environments for self-service, on-demand deployments. 
Chapter-10:  Maintain Pipelines 
  • Monitor pipeline health by tracking failure rates, duration, and flaky tests. 
  • Optimize pipelines for cost, performance, time, and reliability. 
  • Improve pipeline concurrency for better performance and cost efficiency. 
  • Design a retention strategy for managing pipeline artifacts and dependencies to ensure effective management. 
  • Migrate pipelines from classic to YAML in Azure Pipelines. 
Chapter-11:  Design and implement authentication and authorization methods 
  • Decide between Service Principals and Managed Identity (system-assigned and user-assigned). 
  • Implement and manage GitHub authentication using GitHub Apps, GITHUB_TOKEN, and personal access tokens. 
  • Manage Azure DevOps service connections and personal access tokens. 
  • Design and implement permissions and roles in GitHub. 
  • Set up permissions and security groups in Azure DevOps. 
  • Recommend appropriate access levels, including stakeholder and outside collaborator access in Azure DevOps and GitHub. 
  • Configure projects and teams in Azure DevOps. 
Chapter-12:  Design and implement a strategy for managing sensitive information in automation 
  • Manage secrets, keys, and certificates using Azure Key Vault.
  • Handle secrets in GitHub Actions and Azure Pipelines.
  • Design a strategy for managing sensitive files during deployment, including secure files in Azure Pipelines.
  • Design pipelines to prevent the leakage of sensitive information.
Chapter-13:  Implement Automated Scanning for Security and Compliance. 
  • Develop a strategy for security and compliance scanning that includes dependency, code, secret, and license checks.
  • Set up Microsoft Defender for Cloud DevOps Security
  • Set up GitHub Advanced Security for both GitHub and Azure DevOps.
  • Connect GitHub Advanced Security with Microsoft Defender for Cloud
  • Automate container scanning by including container image scans and setting up an action to run CodeQL analysis within a container.
  • Automate the analysis of licensing, vulnerabilities, and versioning for open-source components using Dependabot alerts.
Chapter-14:  Configure monitoring for a DevOps environment 
  • Integrate Azure Monitor and Log Analytics with DevOps tools. 
  • Configure telemetry collection using Application Insights, VM Insights, Container Insights, Storage Insights, and Network Insights. 
  • Set up monitoring in GitHub, including enabling insights and configuring charts. 
  • Configure alerts for events in GitHub Actions and Azure Pipelines. 
Chapter-15:  Analyze metrics from instrumentation 
  • Monitor key infrastructure performance indicators, including CPU, memory, disk, and network usage. 
  • Analyze metrics using collected telemetry, with a focus on usage and application performance. 
  • Use Application Insights for inspecting distributed tracing. 
  • Query logs using basic Kusto Query Language (KQL). 

Note: After the end of the syllabus, there will be question-and-answer preparations for the AZ 900, AZ104, AZ 204, and AZ 400 exams and practice tests. There will also be three live projects that will touch all of the above concepts.

Prerequisites to Join Microsoft Azure Training

No prior experience with Azure is required. However, a basic understanding of Networking Concepts, Operating Systems, and Fundamental Programming Knowledge will enhance your learning experience.

Enroll Today!

The Microsoft Azure Online Training Program offers a comprehensive pathway to mastering Azure fundamentals, administration, and development. With live projects, interactive sessions, and expert guidance, you’ll gain the skills and confidence to excel in cloud computing. Enroll today and take the first step towards becoming a Microsoft Azure expert!

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the duration of the Microsoft Azure Online Training Program?

The training program duration is 5 Months. Daily 2 hours.

Are the training sessions live or pre-recorded?

The training is conducted live by industry experts, allowing for real-time interaction. Recorded sessions are also provided for later review if you miss the live sessions.

Do I need prior experience in cloud computing to join this course?

No prior cloud experience is required for the Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900) exam. However, a basic understanding of IT systems, networking, and programming will benefit students preparing for AZ104, AZ204, and AZ400.

Can I get a demo class before enrolling?

Absolutely! The first three sessions are demo sessions.

How are the live sessions conducted?

The live sessions are conducted via Zoom meetings.

Do I receive a certificate after completing the course?

Yes, you will receive a course completion certificate. You will also be prepared to take official Microsoft Azure certifications.

Are there any live projects included in the training?

Yes, the program includes three live projects covering real-world use cases. These projects involve:

  • Designing Azure solutions.
  • Implementing governance and security.
  • Developing and deploying applications using Azure services.
Does the training include preparation for Microsoft Azure certifications?

Yes, the program is aligned with Microsoft certifications:

  • AZ900: Azure Fundamentals
  • AZ104: Azure Administrator Associate
  • AZ204: Azure Developer Associate
  • AZ400: Azure DevOps

Practice tests and exam preparation sessions are included.

What programming languages are used in the Developer course (AZ204)?

The training primarily focuses on C# and .NET Core, but concepts can be applied to other languages supported by Azure.

Will the course cover interview preparation?

Yes, we provide interview tips, frequently asked questions, and mock interviews to help you excel in your Azure-related job interviews.

What if I miss a live session?

You will have access to recorded sessions for review. You can also clarify doubts during the next session.

Is there a refund policy if I’m unable to continue the training?

Yes, we offer a flexible refund policy. If you decide not to continue, you will receive a full refund within 15 days of enrollment.

Will I get lifetime access to the course materials?

All participants receive lifetime access to course materials, recorded sessions, and other resources.
